metadata.description: MongoDB: Document Database (BSON). Schema-less. Replica Set + Sharding for Horizontal Scaling. Here is an additional explanation with a brief Python code example to illustrate the concept of a document database using PyMongo, a popular Python driver for MongoDB: ```python from pymongo import MongoClient # Connect to the MongoDB server client = MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017/') # Select the database and collection db = client['mydatabase'] collection = db['mycollection'] # Insert a document into the collection (schema-less) document = {"name": "John Doe", "age": 30, "email": "johndoe@example.com"} result = collection.insert_one(document) print(f"Inserted document with ID: {result.inserted_id}") # Query documents from the collection documents = collection.find({"age": 30}) for doc in documents: print(doc) ``` This Python code snippet demonstrates connecting to a MongoDB server, inserting a document into a collection (which is schema-less), and querying documents based on a specific field.
